function.name: list_notion_pages
function.description: Lists all Notion pages with their titles and public visibility status.

function.name: create_notion_page
function.description: Creates a new Notion page. Provide parent_page_id to choose where it goes; otherwise the default from notion-default-page-id in .env is used. If neither is set, Evy will ask for a parent_page_id.

title.description: The title of the new page.

parent_page_id.description: Optional ID of the parent page. If omitted, falls back to notion-default-page-id in .env.

content.description: Optional plain-text content to seed the page with. Each non-empty line becomes a paragraph block.

function.name: get_notion_page_content
function.description: Retrieves the content of a Notion page as readable text. Supports filtering by content type: all blocks, headings only, or todos only.

page_id.description: The ID of the Notion page to retrieve content from.

filter.description: Content filter. 'all' returns the full page, 'headings' returns h1-h3 headings only, 'todos' returns all to-do items only. Defaults to 'all'.

function.name: replace_notion_block
function.description: Finds a block on a Notion page by its exact current text and replaces it with new text. Use get_notion_page_content first to read the exact text of the block you want to change.

page_id.description: The ID of the Notion page containing the block to update.

old_text.description: The exact current text of the block to replace.

new_text.description: The new text to replace it with.

function.name: write_notion_page_content
function.description: Writes content to an existing Notion page. Use 'append' to add to the bottom of the page, or 'replace' to wipe the page and rewrite it entirely.

page_id.description: The ID of the Notion page to write to. Use list_notion_pages to find valid IDs.

content.description: Plain-text content to write. Each non-empty line becomes a paragraph block.

mode.description: 'append' adds content to the bottom of the page. 'replace' clears the page first then writes the new content. Defaults to 'append'.

function.name: list_notion_databases
function.description: Lists all databases in the Notion workspace with their IDs, titles, and property schemas.

function.name: create_notion_database
function.description: Creates a new Notion database (table) under a parent page. The 'properties' parameter defines the columns — pass a JSON object where each key is a property name and each value is an object with 'type' and optional type-specific config. Example: {"Name": {"type": "title"}, "Status": {"type": "select", "options": [{"name": "Todo", "color": "gray"}, {"name": "Done", "color": "green"}]}, "Date": {"type": "date"}}.

parent_page_id.description: The ID of the parent page where the database will be created.

title.description: The title of the new database.

properties.description: Property schema defining the columns. Each key is a property name, each value is an object with 'type' and optional config (e.g. {"Name": {"type": "title"}, "Status": {"type": "select", "select": {"options": [{"name": "Todo", "color": "gray"}]}}}). Supported types: title, rich_text, number, select, multi_select, date, checkbox, email, phone, url, files, status.

function.name: query_notion_database
function.description: Queries a Notion database and returns its items (rows). Supports optional filtering and sorting. Returns each item with its property values.

database_id.description: The ID of the database to query. Use list_notion_databases to find valid IDs.

filter.description: Optional filter conditions as a Notion API filter object (e.g. {"property": "Status", "status": {"equals": "Done"}}). See Notion API docs for full syntax.

page_size.description: Max items to return (1-100). Defaults to 50.

function.name: create_notion_database_item
function.description: Creates a new item (row/page) in a Notion database. The 'properties' parameter should map each property name to its value. IMPORTANT: rich_text and title MUST be ARRAYS of objects, not single objects. Example: {"Name": {"title": [{"text": {"content": "Task 1"}}]}, "Notes": {"rich_text": [{"text": {"content": "Some note"}}]}, "Status": {"select": {"name": "Todo"}}, "Date": {"date": {"start": "2026-07-10"}}}

database_id.description: The ID of the database to add the item to.

properties.description: Property values for the new item. FORMAT RULES: title/rich_text = ARRAY of objects: [{"text": {"content": "..."}}]. select/status = {"name": "Value"}. date = {"start": "YYYY-MM-DD"}. checkbox = true/false. Example: {"Name": {"title": [{"text": {"content": "Task 1"}}]}, "Notes": {"rich_text": [{"text": {"content": "My note"}}]}, "Status": {"select": {"name": "Todo"}}, "Due": {"date": {"start": "2026-07-10"}}}

function.name: update_notion_database_item
function.description: Updates an existing item (page/row) in a Notion database. Provide the page_id of the item and the properties to update. Only the specified properties are changed. IMPORTANT: rich_text and title MUST be ARRAYS of objects, not single objects. Example: {"Status": {"select": {"name": "Done"}}, "Notes": {"rich_text": [{"text": {"content": "Updated note"}}]}}

page_id.description: The ID of the item (page) to update, as returned by query_notion_database.

properties.description: Property values to update. FORMAT RULES: title/rich_text = ARRAY of objects: [{"text": {"content": "..."}}]. select/status = {"name": "Value"}. date = {"start": "YYYY-MM-DD"}. Example: {"Status": {"select": {"name": "Done"}}, "Notes": {"rich_text": [{"text": {"content": "Updated note"}}]}}
